fields.label: EE vendor_c_id
fields.description: EasyEcom READ-side vendor identifier — the `vendor_c_id` returned by /wms/V2/getVendors (e.g. 166334). The join key for this row. UNIQUE at the DB level. Direction-agnostic: the row exists whether the supplier was born in EE (pulled in) or in ERPNext (pushed out).

fields.label: Status
fields.description: §8.3 review/exception state. Mapped = clean; Created-Flagged = soft content issue (e.g. pincode/state mismatch); Flagged-Not-Created = held (e.g. India Compliance rejected GSTIN or PAN format); Drift = post-flip EE-side divergence; Disabled = lifecycle-deactivated. Drives the FDE worklist filter.

fields.label: ERPNext DocType
fields.description: Restricted to 'Supplier' (§8.3). Controller's validate refuses anything else. Dynamic-Link pattern: doctype controls the options for erpnext_name.

fields.label: ERPNext Name
fields.description: The Supplier docname. The pull/push flows write here when they create the corresponding ERPNext Supplier. Linked Billing / Dispatch Address records hang off Supplier via Frappe's standard Address.Dynamic Link.

fields.label: EasyEcom Internal IDs
fields.description: Unlike §8e Customer (where c_id == customerId), §8f Supplier has TWO DISTINCT identifiers: vendor_c_id (read-side, this row's join key in ee_vendor_c_id) and vendor_id (write-side, captured below). Real Harmony data confirms they differ (e.g. 166334 vs 145). PO/STN flows (§9/§10) resolve ERPNext Supplier → EE write key via this row's ee_vendor_id.

fields.label: EE vendor_id (write key)
fields.description: EasyEcom WRITE-side vendor identifier — `vendor_id` (= `vendor_code`, e.g. '145'). Returned by /wms/CreateVendor; consumed by /wms/UpdateVendor as `vendorId`. Populated when push creates the supplier (or when pull observes vendor_code on a customer that was created via portal). Read-only after creation in normal flow; FDE can edit if remapping.

fields.label: Flag Reason
fields.description: For Created-Flagged / Flagged-Not-Created / Drift rows: a plain-English explanation the FDE can act on (e.g. 'ic_pan_invalid_format: bad PAN length', 'ic_gstin_check_digit: failed', 'state/pincode mismatch'). For Drift rows the structured per-field diff lives in the Drift Fields table below; flag_reason summarises ('N fields drifted').

fields.label: Drift Details
fields.description: Populated by the §8.3 post-flip drift detector (Stage 5). One row per field where the incoming EE payload differs from the current ERPNext Supplier / Address state. Cleared when the FDE dismisses (status → Mapped) or pushes ERPNext to EE.

fields.label: Drift Fields
fields.description: Per-field divergence. field=ERPNext field name (e.g. 'gstin', 'pan', 'supplier_name', 'billing.city'), erpnext_value=current ERPNext value, ee_value=what EE asserts (NOT accepted). Reuses §8d/§8e's generic Drift Field child DocType.

fields.label: Dismiss Drift (Mark Reviewed)
fields.description: FDE: the EE-side change is wrong / already-handled-upstream. Returns the row to Mapped and clears the Drift Fields table; the underlying ERPNext Supplier is NOT touched.

fields.label: Push ERPNext → EE (Re-assert SoT)
fields.description: FDE: ERPNext is the source of truth (per the post-flip contract); push current ERPNext Supplier + Addresses to EE via the §8.3 push, overwriting the EE-side divergence.

fields.label: Drift Exclusions
fields.description: Fields the drift detector should IGNORE on this Supplier. Reuses §8d/§8e's generic Exclude Field child DocType. Empty list = compare every field.

fields.label: Push Snapshot
fields.description: Last successful push payload, used to diff against current ERPNext state so the NEXT UpdateVendor sends only the changed fields. Refreshed automatically on every successful push.
